items.title: GitHub Copilot adds an open-weight coding model
items.oneliner: GitHub Copilot now ships Kimi K2.7 Code. Open-weight coding models just entered mainstream enterprise workflows.

items.title: Senior SWE-Bench raises the bar
items.oneliner: Senior SWE-Bench tests runtime debugging, not toy patches. Agent evals are finally inching toward real senior engineering work.

items.title: Mixedbread cuts retrieval storage by 97 percent
items.oneliner: Mixedbread says asymmetric quantization shrinks late-interaction indexes 97% with minor quality loss. Retrieval economics just changed.

items.title: A FUSE bug gives local users root
items.oneliner: CVE-2026-31694 lets unprivileged users gain root on common Linux desktops. FUSE remains a surprisingly sharp local attack surface.

items.title: Browsers may need token-bound sessions
items.oneliner: Token theft keeps bypassing MFA, and browser-generated client certificates may be the most practical fix on the table.

items.title: EU-US data transfers face another legal shock
items.oneliner: A US Supreme Court ruling may undermine FTC independence, putting the EU-US data transfer framework back on thin ice.

items.title: Google loses its Android antitrust appeal
items.oneliner: Europe upheld Google's 4.1 billion euro Android fine. Platform bundling risk is still very real for ecosystem owners.

items.title: Cloudflare turns APIs into metered products
items.oneliner: Cloudflare's x402 gateway brings stablecoin micropayments to APIs and pages. Usage-based monetization is getting infrastructure support.

items.title: Vite Plus bundles the modern web stack
items.oneliner: Vite+ beta packages build, dev, and workflow tooling together. Frontend teams are being pushed toward opinionated consolidation.

items.title: Qualcomm unifies its embedded Linux stack
items.oneliner: Qualcomm Linux 2.0 standardizes Dragonwing IoT on Yocto 6.0 and Linux 6.18 LTS. Embedded fragmentation just took a hit.
